title: Above The Clouds.
content:
And can this be my own world?
        'Tis all gold and snow,
    Save where scarlet waves are hurled
        Down yon gulf below.
    'Tis thy world, 'tis my world,
        City, mead, and shore,
    For he that hath his own world
        Hath many worlds more.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Jean Ingelow, titled "Above The Clouds.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
